unit: U4 — linear-loom events read (synthesizes loom-compat events; closes Phase 6)

contract.goal: Ship linear-loom events read <slug> (+ --since/--event/--limit/--pretty). One combined GraphQL query for Project + Milestones + Sub-Issue comments; synthesize a flat, sorted-by-`at`, loom-compat event array; emit {schema_version: 1, slug, events} JSON. Closes Phase 6.
acceptance_criteria: New cli/lib/events-synthesis.ts: synthesizeFromLinear(input) pure function. Emits project-initialized from Project.createdAt, phase-started per milestone whose name parses, checkin-created per comment matching the U2-rendered header pair. Sorted by `at` ascending.
parseCheckinHeaderFromComment(body) exported alongside; matches the U2 renderer's `**Checkin number**:` + `**Branch**:` header pair (both required for a positive match).

New cli/verbs/events.ts: eventsRead + EVENTS_VERBS. argv parity with loom's events read (--since, --event, --limit, --pretty). Validates --limit (non-negative integer) and --since (parseable ISO) BEFORE Linear round-trip — fail-fast.
Error codes: missing-slug, invalid-limit, invalid-since, linear-project-not-found.
Wired into linear-loom.ts VERB_HANDLERS as events: EVENTS_VERBS.
New contracts/events-read.schema.json: JSON Schema with $defs per event type; partial-coverage scope documented in description; pinned schema_version=1.
Extraction: parsePhaseFromMilestoneName extracted from verbs/project.ts + verbs/phase.ts into new cli/lib/phase-naming.ts (third caller emerged → threshold met per U3's note).
npm test → 900 passing (was 876 after U3; +24 net).
rules_applied: DESIGN.md § 8 (no separate event log; Linear's native audit data IS the event trail; events read synthesizes the loom-compat shape from it).
DESIGN.md § 7 (checkins are Sub-Issue comments — U2's renderer produces the parseable shape U4 reads back).
DESIGN.md § 19 (output shape stability is linear-loom's responsibility; contracts/events-read.schema.json pins the surface).
Operator's grill-me coverage choice this session: trio (Project + Milestones + Comments). PR + state-change history are out-of-scope follow-ups.
disqualifiers: Querying GitHub-PR attachments to synthesize pr-opened / pr-merged events — explicit follow-up; the attachment shape needs investigation work beyond U4's scope.

Querying issueHistory per Sub-Issue to synthesize phase-completed / state-change events — N+1 per-issue queries are too heavy for v1, and Milestone state-change timestamps aren't cleanly exposed in Linear's API at this layer.
Pretty-rendering individual event detail (markdown formatting per event) — the verb emits JSON only.
Heuristic parsing of free-form comments — only U2-rendered checkin comments (with the exact `**Checkin number**:` + `**Branch**:` header pair) become checkin-created.
Mirroring `events latest` (the loom verb's sibling) in this PR — defer to a future unit if a real consumer needs it.
Caching or pagination — the combined query takes whatever Linear returns; > 250 issues in a project is a future Phase 7+ concern.
inputs: plugins/loom/cli/lib/types.ts:59-89 (ProjectInitializedEvent, PhaseStartedEvent, CheckinCreatedEvent shapes — parity reference)
plugins/linear-loom/cli/lib/render-checkin.ts (U2's renderer — produces the parseable shape U4 reads back)
plugins/linear-loom/cli/verbs/phase.ts + verbs/project.ts (U3 + earlier callers of parsePhaseFromMilestoneName — extraction sources)

actions: Extracted parsePhaseFromMilestoneName from verbs/project.ts (declared local) + verbs/phase.ts (duplicated in U3) into new cli/lib/phase-naming.ts. JSDoc names all three callers (project.ts, phase.ts, events-synthesis.ts) and points at the canonical composer in tasks.ts. Both call sites updated to import from the new module.

Wrote cli/lib/events-synthesis.ts: SynthesisInput type (mirrors Linear field-name vocabulary: createdAt, name, body), SynthesizedEvent union with three literal event names. synthesizeFromLinear is pure (no IO, no mutation of input); emits project-initialized then iterates milestones (skipping unparseable names via parsePhaseFromMilestoneName) then iterates issue comments (skipping bodies without the U2 header pair via parseCheckinHeaderFromComment). Final sort by `at` ascending using string-compare (ISO timestamps are lexicographically ordered).

Wrote cli/verbs/events.ts: eventsRead async verb. argv-parity with loom's events read. --limit and --since validated BEFORE marker/auth/Linear round-trip — fail-fast on malformed values. One combined GraphQL query (Project + Milestones + Issues-with-comments, 250 issues / 100 comments per issue defaults). Synthesis runs on the response, then --since / --event / --limit filters apply in the same order loom's events read uses them.

Wired events namespace into linear-loom.ts VERB_HANDLERS as events: EVENTS_VERBS. CLI dispatch: events → missing-verb with candidates ['read']; events read → missing-slug; chained validation for --limit / --since.

Wrote contracts/events-read.schema.json: JSON Schema with $defs for each of the three event types matching loom's detail-field shapes. Top-level shape {schema_version: 1, slug, events}; the description documents the partial-coverage scope (trio shipped; pr-opened/pr-merged/phase-completed deferred to follow-ups). Pinned schema_version=1.

Wrote 24 unit tests: phase-naming.test.ts (6) covers the standard parse + ASCII-hyphen acceptance + null on wrong-slug + null on non-phase + trim trailing whitespace + multi-digit phase numbers. events-synthesis.test.ts (9) covers empty input → project-initialized only + milestone parse → phase-started + unparseable milestone skipped + U2 comment → checkin-created + non-checkin comment skipped + multi-source sort + parseCheckinHeader (positive / one-missing / unrelated). events.test.ts (9) covers all 4 error codes + success + 3 filter modes (--event, --since, --limit) + --pretty.
Verified: npm test → 900 passing across 65 files (was 876 after Phase 6 U3; +24 net). Panel (contract-fit + naming + test-unit): all 3 approved on first run; zero findings.

changes_since_previous: Fourth and final unit of Phase 6 (Manual write-back verbs), stacked on U3 (#47). With this PR merged, Phase 6 closes: ev-linear's loop body (Phase 7) has the full surface it needs — task comment (U1), checkin write (U2), phase update (U3), events read (U4) — to drive a Linear-backed unit-of-work cycle entirely via linear-loom's CLI. Phase 7 (ev-linear plugin) and Phase 8 (Dogfood) remain.
notes_for_pr: U4's third-caller-emerged extraction landed parsePhaseFromMilestoneName into cli/lib/phase-naming.ts. U3's checkin noted this exact threshold (`extract when a third caller emerges`); U4 made it real. Two-site drift risk → zero.

Partial-coverage scope is documented in two places: the events-read.schema.json description names pr-opened / pr-merged / phase-completed as deferred, AND the synthesis lib's leading comment lists the same. Future ev-linear consumers see the trio-only contract pinned, with explicit follow-up names.

Cross-vocab discipline: SynthesisInput mirrors Linear's field-name vocabulary (createdAt) while SynthesizedEvent uses loom's vocabulary (at). The translation lives explicitly in the function body, not in the input/output type shapes — the boundary is honest about what each side speaks.

DESIGN § 7 + U2 + U4 close the bidirectional checkin loop: U2 renders a Checkin JSON to markdown with parseable headers, posts as a comment; U4 reads those comments back and synthesizes checkin-created events. The headers are the contract between the writer (U2) and the reader (U4) — render-checkin.ts:159-175 and events-synthesis.ts:46-58 are the pinned line ranges to keep in lockstep on future renderer changes.
Lagged event note: this commit carries U3's pr-opened event for #47 (substrate-known gap; pr-opened lags the unit commit and rides into the next unit). Same pattern as Phase 6 U2 → U3 → U4.

scope-shift signals: Phase 6's final unit boundary fires (phase-boundary). Single signal — no two-signal concurrence — so no inner-RPI offered. Recording the signal per the loop's single-signal convention.

Substrate follow-up still standing: bin/guild derive-panel returns 'panel-spec-unreadable (using fallback)' under the marketplace-installed CLI. Used node plugins/guild/cli/guild.ts locally throughout Phase 6.
